code:
# Selecting the appropriate adjustment option based on the description
# Given the significant differences in exposure and contrast, we focus on global brightness adjustments.

# Applying the filters in the correct order for global brightness adjustments

# Adjust exposure
f_exp = 0.08  # 8% higher exposure
adjusted_img_00 = filter.exposure(f_exp)

# Adjust contrast
f_cont = 0.075  # 7.5% higher contrast
adjusted_img_00 = filter.contrast(f_cont)
# Given that the exposure and contrast differences are more than 1% as described, focus on global brightness adjustments.

# Load the source image into a variable named 'source_img'
adjusted_img_10 = filter.exposure(f_exp=0.04)  # Adjust exposure to be 4% higher
adjusted_img_10 = filter.contrast(f_cont=0.02)  # Adjust contrast to be 2% higher
# Step 1: Select the appropriate option based on the given difference description
# The description indicates that global brightness adjustments (exposure and contrast) and color tone adjustments (saturation, texture) are needed more than 1%, 
# but highlight and shadow adjustments are not required. So, we choose Global Brightness Adjustment combined with Color Tone and Texture Adjustment.

# Step 2: Apply the appropriate filters in the correct order

# Adjust exposure (brightness)
adjusted_img_22 = filter.exposure(0.02)  # 2% increase in exposure

# Adjust contrast
adjusted_img_22 = filter.contrast(0.02)  # 2% increase in contrast

# Adjust saturation
adjusted_img_22 = filter.saturation(0.02)  # 2% increase in saturation

# Adjust texture
adjusted_img_22 = filter.texture(0.04)  # 4% increase in texture
adjusted_img_30 = filter.exposure(0.01)
adjusted_img_30 = filter.saturation(0.02)
